encounter.reason: new patient thoracic oncology consultation — evaluation of right upper lobe lung mass with mediastinal lymphadenopathy
encounter.service_provider: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
encounter.location: 1515 Holcombe Blvd, Houston, TX 77030 — Thoracic Oncology Clinic

screening.name: ECOG performance status
screening.interpretation: restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out light work

conditions.name: right upper lobe lung mass, suspicious for malignancy
conditions.note: 4.2 cm spiculated mass with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy (stations 4R and 7). Clinical stage at minimum IIIA (T2b N2). Tissue diagnosis pending. First identified on chest X-ray August 1, 2024; CT characterization August 8, 2024. Documented as suspected malignancy; definitive histologic diagnosis not yet established.

conditions.name: essential hypertension
conditions.note: suboptimally controlled today (BP 140/86), likely white coat component. Medication adjustment deferred to PCP.

conditions.name: prediabetes
conditions.note: stable per recent records, no changes at this encounter

conditions.name: mild anemia
conditions.note: hemoglobin 11.6 g/dL, likely multifactorial in setting of suspected malignancy. Plan to trend with subsequent labs.

conditions.name: mild bilateral knee osteoarthritis
conditions.note: intermittent bilateral knee pain, chronic, stable. Mentioned in review of systems and history only.

medications.name: guaifenesin with codeine oral solution
medications.reason: persistent cough associated with right upper lobe lung mass

document_references.description: Comprehensive new patient thoracic oncology consultation documenting HPI (persistent cough, 8 lb unintentional weight loss, referral pathway from PCP through pulmonology), past medical history (hypertension, prediabetes, osteoarthritis), social history (secondhand smoke exposure ~15 pack-years equivalent, retired teacher, never-smoker), family history (no first-degree relatives with lung cancer), medication reconciliation, allergy documentation, complete review of systems, physical exam (decreased breath sounds right upper lobe, no lymphadenopathy, no clubbing), review of outside imaging and PFTs, lab results, assessment of suspected stage IIIA lung malignancy, and staging workup plan including PET/CT, brain MRI, and EBUS biopsy. 75-minute visit with greater than 50% counseling and coordination of care.

diagnostic_reports.description: Albumin 3.3 g/dL (low) and LDH 198 U/L reported individually; remainder described as otherwise unremarkable without individual component values.
